title: Falta repoll results LIVE: Vote counting under way
content: Although officially six candidates are in the fray for the seat, Trinamool Congress nominee Jahangir Khan, a couple of days before the repoll

title: U.P. pushes to strengthen sewerage system, prevent river pollution with multi-pronged approach
content: “Untreated sewage from U.P. cities will no longer flow directly into rivers. Under the Namami Gange Mission Phase-2, sewerage infrastructure is being significantly strengthened,” Additional Chief Secretary, Namami Ganga and Rural Drinking Water Supply Department, UP

title: ITBP officials meet Kanpur top cop seeking action in medical negligence case of jawan’s mother
content: The ITBP personnel accused doctors at a private hospital of negligence that allegedly led to the amputation of his mother’s right hand; he demanded a fair investigation and strict action against the hospital administration and the doctors involved.

title: PM Modi’s jhalmuri break in Jhargram leaves Trinamool fuming
content: Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren and his wife Kalpana Soren had to return after waiting for hours to campaign in Jhargram as they were not allowed to land, the party says

title: Security stepped up at Jhalmuri stall which PM visited during WB election campaign
content: Bikram Kumar Sau, the Jhalmuri vendor, lodged a written complaint at the Jhargram police station, alleging that he had been receiving threatening phone calls and messages from people threatening to kill him.

title: Kasaragod district administration prepares railway master plan to end travel hardships
content: District Collector Arjun Pandian has formally written to railway authorities seeking the extension of major train services terminating at Kannur and Mangaluru to Kasaragod, restoration of discontinued passenger services, and stoppages for key superfast trains at Kasaragod railway station

title: Counting of votes in Bengal’s Falta Assembly repoll begins
content: "The counting is progressing peacefully. Adequate security arrangements have been made at the counting centre," a poll panel official said.

title: Manipur CM assures NIA probe into abduction of Naga men
content: Kuki Inpi Manipur extends shutdown since May 13 by another 48 hours to secure release of 14 Kuki people

title: What did the Supreme Court say about bail under UAPA?
content: What are the disagreements between recent Supreme Court rulings on UAPA bail? Why is bail so difficult under Section 43D(5) of the UAPA? What did the Court say in K.A. Najeeb about prolonged incarceration and bail? What role does Article 21 play in UAPA bail jurisprudence?

title: A nation that makes its own weapons writes its destiny: Rajnath Singh
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said future wars would increasingly be determined by advancements in munitions, automation, and emerging technologies rather than merely the size of armed forces

title: Supreme Court registers suo motu case over death of Twisha Sharma
content: A three-judge Bench comprising Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M. Pancholi is scheduled to hear the matter on Monday (May 25)

title: Surya Kiran Aerobatic Team to celebrate 30th anniversary on May 26 at Bidar
content: Since 2015, the team has been operating the Hawk Mk-132 aircraft in its signature red-and-white colour scheme and nine-aircraft formation

title: News in Frames | The drying naulas of Uttarakhand
content: Many of the naulas and dharas, the water springs which represent a union of nature and human ingenuity, are drying up in signs of a deepening environmental crisis across Uttarakhand

title: BJP President Nitin Nabin’s Birthday: Melody chocolates distributed among visually impaired children in Patna
content: Mr. Nabin was in Patna on May 23, 2026, and welcomed a few party leaders at his residence who had come to wish him on his 46th birthday

title: Surviving a city on ₹13,690
content: The Uttar Pradesh government increased its minimum wage after 12 years by ₹2,377 last month. While workers are marginally relieved, those in Noida’s SEZ say the amount still barely sustains them, and there is little left to save. Shrimansi Kaushik finds that workers’ spaces and aspirations have shrunk over time

title: West Bengal gained infamy due to recruitment scams, new recruitment policy in next Budget session: CM
content: The allegation of corruption in government jobs came to hurt the Trinamool Congress and the party lost the recently concluded assembly elections to the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP).

title: Why did Kerala scrap the SilverLine project?
content: What was the project and why was it projected as a game-changer for Kerala’s transport sector? Why did the land acquisition process trigger protests? What processes were put in place to acquire land? What were the concerns over the project?

title: ₹350 crore investment to generate 2,300 employment opportunities in Bihar
content: Industries Minister Shreyasi Singh said that the newly approved industrial projects are set to provide fresh momentum to Bihar’s industrial and technological development

title: How has the BCCI resisted RTI scrutiny?
content: Why does the body not qualify as a public authority? What does the CIC order say? What are the implications?

title: Frequent disruption of ro-ro services give harrowing time to commuters
content: Owing to the delay in inducting the third ro-ro ferry, demand is rife that the Kochi Corporation impose curbs on transporting goods carriers on the Fort Kochi-Vypeen stretch during peak hours

title: DMK, Congress trade barbs over the latter’s post-poll alliance with the TVK
content: Speaking at a DMK youth wing meeting, Leader of the Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin slams the Congress and says the national party is responsible for the BJP’s continuous victories in the country and alleges that the DMK has carried such a party on its shoulders in the Lok Sabha and recent Assembly elections

title: TVK government will fall one day, says M.K. Stalin
content: Addressing the party’s youth wing meeting, he asserts that what has happened in T.N. is “not a political tsunami, but a cinema tsunami” and likenes public support for Vijay to a child’s fascination with a new doll

title: Twisha Sharma death case: husband Samarth Singh brought to Bhopal, police to seek remand
content: Bhopal Commissioner said local police would continue the investigation until the central agency formally takes over the case; yet to be reached for recording my statement, says Giribala Singh

title: TVK regime looks to maximise revenue without increasing taxes
content: While the ruling party, in its manifesto, has promised several welfare measures and freebies to people, it is also getting conscious of the fiscal position in which the government finds itself in

title: Power cuts caused by load issues, not shortage, says Minister R. Nirmalkumar
content: He says the Electricity Department has constituted seven rapid response teams of engineers in Chennai, and they have been provided vehicles to swiftly reach spots and rectify complaints

title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked again in Bengaluru; transport sector warns of ripple effect
content: Oil companies had earlier revised prices upward by around ₹3 per litre on May 15, followed by another 90 paise increase on May 19

title: I don’t think the ruling party has the stomach for sarcasm, says Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke
content: Abhijeet Dipke, founder of the ‘Cockroach Janta Party’, alleges threats and censorship following the takedown of his youth-led satirical political outfit’s website

title: Fuel price hike: Bengaluru hoteliers feel the heat
content: Almost everything restaurants depend on- vegetables, milk, meat, groceries, cooking supplies, and even laundry materials- arrives through transport networks dependent on diesel.

title: Speakers at leadership summit emphasise on sustained deliberation on role of AI in critical sectors
content: Pro Chancellor of KLE Technological University Prof. Ashok Shettar has emphasised the need for a sustained deliberation on the role of Artificial Intelligence and its implications across critical sectors such as education, banking, defence, and healthcare

title: Hubballi Dharwad Central MLA  tells officials to facilitate export of mangoes grown in region
content: Emphasising the need for facilitating export of the mangoes grown by the farmers of the region, Hubballi Dharwad Central MLA Mahesh Tenginakai has said the officials should provide requisite information and handhold the farmers initially

title: Centre committed to ensuring transparent, corruption-free recruitmentm says Union Minister Joshi
content: Union Minister for Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution Pralhad Joshi has said that the union government was committed to ensuring transparent and corruption-free recruitment processes.

title: KSRTC driver suspended over defamatory post against Kerala Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan
content: Transport corporation initiates departmental disciplinary action against the driver attached to the KSRTC Nedumkandam unit in Idukki

title: Karnataka to soon get India’s second largest international cricket stadium
content: The move comes despite opposition from a section of farmers and environmentalists who say the proposed stadium, which will come up close to the Karadikkal-Mahadeshwara wildlife corridor, will have an adverse impact on the environment.

title: A ship-sized hole in the budget
content: Fuel costs have risen steeply, thrice in the span of 10 days, as the Iran war continues to choke supplies globally. This has also led to an increase in the prices across the board, with grocery, food, and commute rates burgeoning. Many people in Tamil Nadu find the going very difficult

title: CPI(M) demands scrapping NTA, seeks Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation
content: The party claimed that since the NTA was set up in 2017, "there have been at least four instances when leakages were exposed" and alleged that the agency had been plagued by multiple problems while conducting examinations

title: Marco Rubio in India highlights: PM Modi, Rubio discuss bilateral ties, West Asia crisis
content: Rubio invites PM Modi to White House; they discussed India-U.S. Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership and issues related to regional and global peace and security

title: Agri-tech has improved productivity and post-harvest management, say experts at webinar
content: An expert said that 47% of farm work was now mechanised, and that the introduction of scientific methods has led to surplus production

title: Cabinet clears 2.5 lakh houses, ₹587 crore for PRLSI
content: State to join RDSS and ₹300 crore sanctioned as first instalment for preparations for Godavari Pushkarams, mid-day meals in junior colleges

title: Twisha Sharma’s husband sent to 7-day police remand; AIIMS Delhi forms panel for second autopsy
content: Police told The Hindu that during the remand period, the SIT probing the case will recreate the scene of crime along with seizing substantial evidence from him and interrogating him on various aspects

title: MHA warns of phishing campaign targeting users of lost or stolen iPhones
content: Hackers impersonate Apple Support and exploit victims’ urgency to locate or secure their missing devices through fraudulent SMS messages containing phishing links

title: White House shooter Nasire Best, 21, who identified himself as Jesus Christ, shot dead
content: A 21-year-old man, Nasire Best, who allegedly believed he was Jesus Christ, was killed by Secret Service after opening fire outside the White House. Best had a history of loitering and violating orders to stay away from the complex. The incident triggered a lockdown and injured at least one bystander.

title: Delhi weather: Relief short-lived, brace for heatwave spell from today
content: A thunderstorm hit Delhi with strong winds, offering brief relief from the heat. Temperatures are rising again, and heatwave conditions are forecast to return from Sunday. Maximum temperatures are expected to increase by 2-3 degrees Celsius over the next four days. Delhi's air quality remains in the moderate category.

title: 'Well done, Arjun': Sachin pens emotional note after son’s IPL 2026 debut
content: Arjun Tendulkar impressed on his IPL debut for Lucknow Super Giants with figures of 4-0-36-1 against Punjab Kings, including the wicket of Prabhsimran Singh. Proud father Sachin Tendulkar shared a rare emotional message, praising Arjun’s patience, hard work, and attitude after waiting all season for an opportunity in IPL 2026.

title: '94% audience from India': Cockroach Janta Party founder on BJP's Pakistan-followers charge
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke refutes BJP claims of Pakistani followers. He presented audience analytics showing over 94% Indian users. Union minister Kiren Rijiju and BJP leader Sukanta Majumdar had alleged significant Pakistani engagement. Kerala BJP president Rajeev Chandrasekhar called it a cross-border influence operation. The party gained traction online discussing unemployment and policy issues.

title: CEO Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta staff has 2 promises for survivors
content: Mark Zuckerberg's goodbye memo to 8,000 fired Meta employees on May 20 doubled as a message to the 70,000 staff still standing. The Meta CEO made two promises: no further company-wide layoffs in 2026, and better communication after weeks of internal anxiety. The memo lands as Meta funnels $125-145 billion into AI infrastructure, shifts 7,000 staff to AI projects, and cancels 6,000 open roles.

title: ‘Sent phone password before death’: Bengaluru cop’s wife found dead at police quarters
content: A 26-year-old woman, Lakshmi Priya, died at her residence in Bengaluru. Her family claims her husband and in-laws subjected her to harassment. Priya's sister received a suspicious message before her death. Her husband reported she died by suicide. Police have registered a case against the husband and his family for dowry death and cruelty.

title: 'A genuine human error': Ram Charan apologises to Jasprit Bumrah
content: Actor Ram Charan went viral after mistakenly mentioning football while praising Jasprit Bumrah at Peddi’s music launch in Bhopal. He later apologised, calling it a “genuine human error.” Meanwhile, Mumbai Indians coach Mahela Jayawardene revealed Bumrah battled an injury during IPL 2026 but said the pacer has regained rhythm and confidence in recent matches.

title: Defence partnership: India conducts specialised military training for Oman forces
content: India and Oman have significantly bolstered their defence ties with specialized military training for Omani forces.  This initiative, involving Indian Army, Navy, and Air Force experts, focused on enhancing joint operational coordination and logistics.  The move underscores deepening strategic cooperation amidst regional security concerns and follows recent discussions on West Asia.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to California group, and this is why
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: Ugly scenes! Fans misbehave with cheerleaders; police intervene - Watch
content: Punjab Kings secured a crucial seven-wicket victory against Lucknow Super Giants, keeping their IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive. Captain Shreyas Iyer's maiden IPL century guided the team to a commanding chase. The match also saw an unfortunate incident where fans allegedly misbehaved with cheerleaders, prompting police intervention. Punjab now awaits the outcome of other matches to confirm their playoff spot.

title: Iran agrees to give up enriched uranium as deal with Trump 'largely negotiated': Report
content: Iran has reportedly agreed in principle to surrender its enriched uranium stockpile to the US as part of a peace deal. This significant concession, aimed at de-escalating tensions and reopening the Strait of Hormuz, comes after US military threats. While a deal is "largely negotiated," final details on the uranium transfer and broader nuclear talks are pending.

title: Jeff Bezos to Amazon users: We can’t run Amazon the way NYC runs its school system
content: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has sharply criticized New York City's public school spending, likening its inefficiencies to a poorly run business. He argued that substantial funds aren't reaching teachers, despite high per-student costs. Bezos also dismissed 'tax-the-rich' policies, advocating for identifying and solving root causes of economic struggles instead.

title: Twisha death case: CBI to probe final chats, injuries, alleged abortion angle as 'trigger point'
content: The CBI's investigation into Twisha Sharma's death will focus on a "trigger point" preceding her distress messages. Investigators will reconstruct her final days, examining if a specific event coincided with her alleged messages of suffocation and trauma, alongside MTP records and forensic findings.

title: Salman Khan's '7 Dogs' cameo goes viral after Egypt premiere
content: Salman Khan's explosive cameo in the global suspense thriller '7 Dogs' has sent shockwaves through social media after its premiere in Egypt. Although he didn't attend, the event was buzzing with oversized posters and a hilarious barber shop scene featuring the Bollywood icon speaking English, much to the delight of fans and adding tremendous hype to the film.

title: Donald Trump opposes Jaxson Dart playing against women: Report
content: During a campaign event, US President Donald Trump unexpectedly praised New York Giants quarterback Jaxson Dart’s physique. He reportedly linked it to his stance against transgender athletes in women’s sports. Trump questioned if any woman could tackle Dart. He highlighted Dart’s muscular build. The comments drew mixed reactions online, with some criticizing the focus on physical appearance over politics.

title: Kylian Mbappe’s Real Madrid future in focus amid Chelsea rumors
content: Kylian Mbappe has been linked with a surprise move to Chelsea after another frustrating season at Real Madrid despite his excellent goal record. Football expert Andy Brassell believes Mbappe’s strong connection with Xabi Alonso could play a key role in the future. Chelsea were interested in Mbappe during his youth years, and fresh transfer speculation has now returned again.

title: Toronto Maple Leafs face uncertainty amid Matthew Knies trade rumors
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs have several offseason questions to answer, and the trade talk surrounding forward Matthew Knies is heating up. Knies is still young and promising, but there is speculation that he might be used in a major roster move. Meanwhile, San Jose Sharks defenceman Mario Ferraro has emerged as a possible defensive target, adding to the complexity of Toronto’s NHL offseason plans.

title: Taiwan detects Chinese military aircraft, naval vessels near island for second straight day
content: Taiwan's defense ministry reported Chinese military activity, with four aircraft and six naval vessels detected around the island. Three aircraft crossed the Taiwan Strait median line. This follows a day of heightened activity, with 16 aircraft and eight vessels observed, 13 of which crossed the median line. These incursions underscore rising regional tensions.

title: IPL 2026 Super Sunday: One team waits, two chase final playoff spot
content: IPL 2026's league stage culminates in a thrilling finale with one playoff spot undecided. Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Gujarat Titans lead, while Sunrisers Hyderabad secure third. Rajasthan Royals, Kolkata Knight Riders, and Punjab Kings vie for the final berth. Super Sunday's matches will determine which two dreams end and one advances.

title: Who is Ansiba Hassan? The face behind 'Drishyam's Anju George
content: Ansiba Hassan, the elder daughter from the Drishyam franchise, is back in the spotlight. Her acting journey, fueled by her mother's unfulfilled dream, began with a reality show and early Tamil films before landing the pivotal role in 'Drishyam'.  Despite recent controversies with AMMA, her career continues to thrive with 'Drishyam 3' now in theatres.

title: Next 24 Hours Crucial: Shoaib Ibrahim says father suffers another stroke
content: Shoaib Ibrahim's father has been hospitalized in the ICU following another brain stroke, with the next 24 hours deemed critical. This follows a previous stroke in 2021 that left him partially paralyzed. The family has faced recent health challenges, including Dipika Kakar's successful battle with liver cancer and subsequent cyst diagnosis.

title: ‘Virat Kohli should have shaken hands’: Ex-India cricketer on Travis Head controversy
content: Former India opener Wasim Jaffer believes Virat Kohli should have moved past his heated exchange with Travis Head after the match. Jaffer emphasized Kohli's role as a global ambassador and stated that post-match interactions should remain respectful. He also suggested that RCB's heavy defeat could serve as a wake-up call before the playoffs.

title: 'We support MI': Iyer's message goes viral after Punjab Kings win
content: Shreyas Iyer's maiden IPL century powered Punjab Kings to a crucial seven-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants, keeping their playoff hopes alive. Iyer's magnificent 100, coupled with a 140-run partnership with Prabhsimran Singh, guided PBKS to 15 points. Their playoff fate now hinges on the Rajasthan Royals vs. Mumbai Indians match.

title: Strong El Nino may peak this year, weaken before India’s 2027 monsoon
content: Meteorologists suggest strong El Niño events typically weaken significantly before India's monsoon season. Historical data from powerful El Niños like 1997-98 and 2015-16 show a pattern of peaking in winter and then subsiding. This trend offers reassurance regarding the 2027 monsoon, as a weakening El Niño or a shift to La Niña is anticipated.

title: Odisha fugitive missing for 32 years found in cellar hideout
content: A 32-year manhunt concluded with the arrest of Rabindra Mallick, accused of a 1994 murder. Police discovered him in a secret underground chamber at his Jajpur home after he returned for a family event. Mallick had lived under false identities and worked as a mason in various states, evading capture for decades. His bail plea was denied.

title: Tamil Nadu minor girl murder: CM Vijay orders fast-track probe, vows strict punishment
content: Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay has ordered a swift and intensive investigation into the abduction and murder of a 10-year-old girl in Coimbatore. Assuring severe punishment for those involved in crimes against women and children, he stated that two individuals have been arrested and a speedy probe is underway to file the chargesheet immediately.

title: NEET paper leak: Arrested headmistress recalled physics questions from memory, says CBI
content: A school headmistress in Pune, Manisha Havaldar, has been arrested by the CBI for allegedly reconstructing NEET physics questions from memory and sharing them with a student. She claimed to have acted on instructions from an NTA chemistry expert and later circulated the questions via a messaging app.

title: US, Iran move closer to 60-day ceasefire extension: What's in the deal
content: The US and Iran are reportedly nearing a 60-day ceasefire extension, with mediators working on an interim deal. This agreement could pave the way for broader talks on Iran's nuclear program and regional security, including the gradual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and phased sanctions relief for Tehran.

title: US-Iran Talks Close To Peace Deal? Trump Says "Largely Negotiated"
content: Trump, in a post on Truth Social, also said that the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened as a part of the proposed understanding.

title: White House Shooter Was Arrested In 2025, Believed He Was 'Jesus Christ'
content: Nasire Best was allegedly "mentally troubled" and was known to the US security officials for loitering around many entry posts.

title: Bengaluru Woman, 26, Dies By Suicide, Family Alleges Harassment By In-Laws
content: The complainant alleged that after the marriage, Rajesh Aradhya, along with his father Palaksharadhya and mother Bhagyamma, subjected Lakshmi Priya to continuous mental and physical harassment.

title: Women To Travel Free On All State-Run Buses In West Bengal From June 1
content: The notification stated that "free transportation shall be provided in all state-run buses to all women of West Bengal," covering "all short-route and long-route state-run buses"

title: Video: Journalists Duck For Cover As Gunman Opens Fire Outside White House
content: Reporters took cover as shots were heard outside the White House, and were later rushed to the press briefing room.

title: Live Updates: Shooting Outside White House, Suspect 'Removed Gun From Bag, Fired At Officers'
content: White House Shooting live updates: Both individuals were said to be in critical condition, according to the official, who spoke on the condition of anonymity because they were not authorised to discuss the investigation.

title: Karnataka Woman Dies By Suicide, Alleges Dowry Harassment By Vet Husband
content: Aishwarya's death was reported from Kampli town, days after a similar case of the death of a newly married woman, Twisha Sharma, left the nation in shock.

title: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: "Iran War Will Be Solved One Way Or The Other": Marco Rubio To NDTV
content: Marco Rubio India Visit Live Updates: The top US diplomat is scheduled to hold talks with External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar and attend a meeting of the Quad foreign ministers.

title: On Trinamool Turncoats In Bengal, BJP Between A Rock And A Hard Place
content: The BJP's state unit has bluntly declined to give way to prospective Trinamool turncoats, arguing the party isn't a dharmshala.

title: Refrain From Cow Slaughter: Assam Eid Committees Ahead Of Bakri Eid
content: The appeal from the committees in Assam, which has 35 per cent Muslim population, came following an appeal by Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma on Saturday.

title: Jaipur Woman Dies By Suicide After Domestic Abuse, Children Recount Torture
content: Days after the incident, Mahir accessed his mother's phone and retrieved several videos, including footage of an altercation.

title: Dharmendra Pradhan Seeks CBSE Report Over Re-Evaluation Glitches
content: Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan has sought a detailed report from the CBSE over complaints of technical glitches faced by students and parents during the re-evaluation process, a source said on Saturday.

title: After PM's 'Satisfactory' Appraisal Rating, Ministers Swing Into Action
content: The chief yardstick for the performance appraisal was the ministries' agility in complaint redressal and file disposal.

title: 'US Not Honest, Iran Won't Compromise In Talks': Top Negotiator To Pak
content: Iran's top negotiator Mohammad Baqer Ghalibaf told Pakistani army chief Asim Munir during talks in Tehran that the US was not an honest party in negotiations.

title: Trump Says Iran Deal Has "Solid 50/50" Chance, Warns Of Strikes If Talks Fail
content: Trump also warned that if the US and Iran do not come to an agreement, "we're going to have a situation where no country will ever be hit as hard as they're about to be hit."

title: NIA To Take Up Cases Of 3 Thadou Leaders Killing, 6 Missing Nagas In Manipur
content: Search and rescue operations have been launched since May 14 to trace the whereabouts of the six Naga villagers, Manipur Chief Minister Yumnam Khemchand Singh said

title: India Eyes $50 Billion Trade Goal As Piyush Goyal Set For Canada Visit
content: Goyal will be accompanied by senior executives from more than 100 Indian companies representing sectors such as energy, metals and mining, aerospace, telecom, pharmaceuticals, agriculture, tourism, textiles, automotive, and capital goods.

title: Delhi Heatwave Back After Brief Relief, Weather Office Issues Warning
content: Residents breathed a collective sigh of relief on Saturday as strong winds, thunderstorms, lightning, and scattered rainfall swept Delhi.

title: Amid fuel price hikes, states urged to cut VAT
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: Site, web accounts blocked, says Cockroach Janta Party founder
content: founder abhijeet Dipke says the website was taken down by the centre . he says the govt has taken down our iconic website . Dipke alleged the site was pulled down after the movement gained traction online .

title: Leh chopper crash: Major General poses for selfie after accident, pilot flashes victory sign
content: a major general, a Lieutenant Colonel and a Major survived the crash . the helicopter crashed in the mountainous Tangste near leh in ladakh . a selfie by one of the officers of the crash has now gone viral .

title: Rubio meets PM Modi, delivers Trump's invite to visit US in ‘near future’
content: the two sides discussed the situation in west Asia, according to a readout . Rubio emphasised that the US will not let Iran hold the global energy market hostage and affirmed that US energy products have the potential to diversify India’s energy supply .

title: Only 6 states now have fertility rate above replacement level
content: india's total fertility rate (TFR), or the average number of children a woman would have, has fallen even further to 1.9 from 2.1, according to the latest sample registration system (SRS) report of 2024 . the TFR has fallen below the replacement level for all other states .

title: 'Malicious propaganda': Army says soldiers in 'fake' Press Club video dismissed or facing action
content: the video of a press conference at the press club of india was circulated on various media and social media platforms . it alleges that it was a malicious propaganda campaign designed to malign the force’s image and push fabricated narratives . the Indian Army clarified that the individuals seen in the viral clip had been dismissed from service on grounds of indiscipline and unsoldierly conduct .

title: Delhi's Gymkhana Club near PM residence told to vacate over national security needs
content: govt had given 27.3 acres of land on perpetual lease to DGC in 1928 . the club is located next to PM’s residence in Lutyens’ Delhi . it has been operated from 2, Safdarjung Road since 1913 .

title: 217 Indians joined Russian army, 49 dead, Centre tells SC
content: as many as 217 Indians have joined Russian forces, of which 49 died and six are missing in action in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war . the govt said the Indian Embassy is making efforts to bring back the 139 people who have been released from military service .

title: Gunshots heard near White House during Donald Trump’s Iran talks
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. title: ‘Reward for theft?’ Kerala oppn targets UDF as poll officer appointed CM’s secretary
content: both BJP and BJP have come out against the state govt's decision to appoint chief electoral officer Rathan Khelkar as chief minister V D Satheesan's principal secretary . the state secretariat says the appointment is a step that calls into question the credibility of the assembly elections itself . former state president K Surendran was the first to raise the alleged double standard of Congress .

title: Fuel prices up 3rd time in 10 days, PSUs losing Rs 13/litre on petrol, Rs 38 on diesel
content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines. title: Green card? H-1Bs must prove worthy of ‘status adjustment’
content: Lubna Kably is a senior editor, who focuses on various policies and legislation. In particular, she writes extensively on immigration and tax policies. The Indian diaspora is the largest in the world; through her articles she demystifies the immigration-policy related developments in select countries for outbound students, job aspirants and employees. She also analyses the impact of Income-tax and GST related developments for individuals and business entities. Read More
